incidents.victim: Aqua Security, Checkmarx, LiteLLM, Telnyx (software supply chain)
incidents.vector: Compromised GitHub Actions and PyPI packages via stolen maintainer credentials, deploying credential-harvesting malware through trusted open-source tooling including Trivy, KICS, LiteLLM, and Telnyx SDK.
incidents.impact: Estimated 300 GB of credentials stolen from 1,000+ impacted SaaS environments. Mandiant warns downstream victims could reach 10,000+. Group partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service and distributed affiliate keys to ~300,000 BreachForums users. CISA added CVE-2026-33634 to KEV catalog with April 8 remediation deadline. Campaign entered monetization phase with first 48-hour pause in new compromises as of March 28.

incidents.victim: Stryker Corporation
incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune administrative account and used the device management system's native wipe functionality to remotely wipe over 200,000 company devices globally. Malware was also used to hide attacker activity.
incidents.impact: Hospital operations disrupted in Maryland; some surgeries cancelled due to unavailable implants. Production lines offline for two weeks, now ramping back up. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 confirmed threat actor removed and persistence mechanisms cleared. FBI seized Handala group domains. DOJ affidavit cited direct impact on emergency medical services.

incidents.victim: Organizations using F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2025-53521 (CVSS 9.8), reclassified from DoS to remote code execution via malicious traffic to BIG-IP APM virtual servers. Originally disclosed October 2025.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog March 27. Malicious files deployed on compromised systems including modified system binaries. Mass scanning activity observed; honeypots under consistent attack with new fingerprinting techniques emerging.

incidents.victim: Organizations in education, healthcare, engineering, and government
incidents.vector: Exploited critical zero-day in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (CVE-2026-20131) for remote code execution weeks before public disclosure on March 4.
incidents.impact: Attackers had zero-day access from January 26, deploying custom malware, reconnaissance scripts, and webshells across multiple victim networks. Interlock operators work in UTC+3 timezone. Previous targets include St. Paul, Minnesota government and DaVita dialysis company.

incidents.victim: Enterprise organizations using Cisco SD-WAN
incidents.vector: Exploitation of multiple zero-day vulnerabilities in Cisco SD-WAN systems that went undetected for over three years; five of nine disclosed vulnerabilities confirmed actively exploited.
incidents.impact: Network management and control-plane infrastructure compromised at enterprise scale. CISA added two vulnerabilities to KEV catalog and issued emergency directive. Attackers maintained persistent access to network edge infrastructure across multiple sectors.

incidents.victim: iOS device users (Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, Ukraine)
incidents.attacker: Multiple state-linked espionage groups and commercial surveillance vendors
incidents.vector: Full-chain iOS exploit kit (DarkSword) leveraging six zero-day vulnerabilities across iOS 18.4-18.7 to deploy surveillance malware families. GitHub leak of exploit code threatens to commoditize access.
incidents.impact: Victims in at least four countries subjected to full device compromise including message interception, location tracking, and microphone/camera access. Multiple state actors including suspected Russian espionage operators adopted the chain. Apple patched in iOS 26.3.

incidents.victim: Southeast Asian military organizations
incidents.vector: Years-long cyber espionage campaign using novel backdoors and credential-stealing tools with persistent footholds across military networks. Multiple overlapping campaigns (CL-STA-1048, CL-STA-1049, CL-STA-1087) deployed against the same government targets.
incidents.impact: Strategic military intelligence collected over multiple years from Southeast Asian defense organizations. Multiple RAT families deployed in parallel campaigns.

incidents.victim: Global telecommunications providers
incidents.vector: Upgraded BPFdoor backdoor malware using Berkeley Packet Filters for covert persistence; defeats traditional cybersecurity protections by operating at kernel level.
incidents.impact: Active backdoor access to telecom infrastructure globally. The advanced implant makes detection extremely difficult; defenders must resort to proactive threat hunting to identify compromised systems.

incidents.victim: Hundreds of organizations (construction, law, nonprofits, real estate, manufacturing, healthcare)
incidents.vector: AI-generated phishing emails exploiting Microsoft device authentication OAuth flow via Railway platform infrastructure; unique lures including QR codes bypass email security filters at scale.
incidents.impact: 344+ confirmed victims with 50+ new compromises per day. Attackers obtain 90-day OAuth tokens bypassing MFA entirely. Huntress pushed emergency conditional access policy update to 60,000 Microsoft cloud tenants.

incidents.victim: Organizations using FortiClient Endpoint Management Server
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-21643, a critical SQL injection flaw in FortiClient EMS allowing remote code execution via crafted SQL statements in HTTP request headers.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed via honeypot data. Close to 1,000 FortiClient EMS instances publicly exposed via Shodan. Dedicated attacker infrastructure targeting FortiClient exclusively.

incidents.victim: Unnamed US healthcare organization
incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware deployed via compromised administrative account; attackers cleared all traces of activity and event logs after encryption.
incidents.impact: Healthcare operations disrupted; no data exfiltration detected. Destructive approach suggests strategic motivations beyond financial gain amid Iran military conflict. Pay2Key has accumulated 170 victims and $8M in ransoms since summer 2025.

incidents.victim: Langflow platform users
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8) — unauthenticated code injection in Langflow API endpoint allowing arbitrary Python execution without sandboxing.
incidents.impact: Attackers deploying info stealers, reverse shells, and cryptominers. API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, and AWS extracted from compromised instances. CISA added to KEV catalog March 25 alongside Trivy CVE, signaling AI toolchain security treated as systemic risk.

incidents.victim: Israeli Defense Force personnel, Sanzer Hasidic Jewish community
incidents.vector: Stole PII and sensitive data on ~190 IDF-associated individuals and 851 GB of confidential data from Sanzer community members; posted data with threatening messages on Handala Hack domains.
incidents.impact: Personal information, financial documents, and private correspondence exposed with explicit threats including location/residence details and $250,000 bounty offers. FBI seized four domains on March 19. Death threats sent to Iranian dissidents and journalists.

incidents.victim: Iranian dissidents and journalists worldwide
incidents.vector: Custom malware masquerading as legitimate applications (Pictory, KeePass, Telegram) delivered via social engineering; uses Telegram bot as C2 infrastructure. Campaign active since 2023.
incidents.impact: Full remote access to victim devices including screen capture, audio recording, file exfiltration, and location tracking. FBI issued alert escalating urgency due to ongoing US-Iran conflict. Malware tailored to each victim's pattern of life.

incidents.victim: Organizations in Iran (Kubernetes clusters)
incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) spreading through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers; executes data destruction on systems matching Iran timezone or Farsi language settings.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on all nodes in compromised Kubernetes clusters. Uses Internet Computer Protocol canisters for tamperproof C2. Shares infrastructure with the broader TeamPCP supply chain campaign.

incidents.victim: AstraZeneca (unconfirmed)
incidents.vector: Attackers claimed to have used credentials stolen from the TeamPCP supply chain campaign to breach internal systems; offering access for sale via encrypted messaging.
incidents.impact: Claimed 3 GB breach including internal code repositories, AWS/Azure configurations, GitHub Enterprise user data, and employee information. Breach unconfirmed at 48+ hours — AstraZeneca has not issued a public statement.

incidents.victim: Ubiquiti UniFi Networking Application users (88,000+ exposed instances)
incidents.vector: Maximum-severity path-traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-22557, CVSS 10) in UniFi Network Application allowing unauthenticated account takeover and privilege escalation.
incidents.impact: 88,000+ instances publicly exposed on the internet, one-third in the United States. Exploitation expected to be trivial to automate once vulnerable endpoint is identified. Patches available.

incidents.victim: Trio-Tech International (Singapore subsidiary)
incidents.vector: Ransomware attack encrypting files within subsidiary network; escalated on March 18 with unauthorized disclosure of company data.
incidents.impact: Company reported material cybersecurity event to SEC. Systems taken offline; restoration ongoing. Affected parties being notified. Trio-Tech has $36M revenue and 600 employees across US and Asia.

incidents.victim: Ransomware victims across multiple US companies
incidents.vector: Aleksei Volkov (26, St. Petersburg) operated as initial access broker — identified vulnerabilities, gained unauthorized access to corporate networks, and sold access to ransomware groups including Yanluowang.
incidents.impact: Volkov sentenced to 81 months in federal prison on March 23. Facilitated dozens of ransomware attacks causing $9 million in actual losses and $24 million in intended losses. Extradited from Italy.

incidents.victim: Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service platform users and targets
incidents.vector: Tycoon2FA adversary-in-the-middle phishing platform persisted after law enforcement takedown attempt by rapidly rebuilding infrastructure and continuing credential theft operations.
incidents.impact: Platform continues enabling MFA-bypass phishing attacks at scale. Demonstrates resilience of phishing-as-a-service operations against enforcement actions.

incidents.victim: SideWinder espionage targets (Southeast Asian governments, telecom, critical infrastructure)
incidents.vector: Spear-phishing emails exploiting old vulnerabilities combined with rapidly rotating infrastructure to maintain persistent access across government and telecom networks.
incidents.impact: Expanded espionage campaign targeting governments, telecommunications, and critical infrastructure across Southeast Asia. Rapidly rotating infrastructure makes takedown difficult.

incidents.victim: Sears (customer data exposed)
incidents.vector: Three publicly exposed, unprotected databases discovered containing Sears AI chatbot data.
incidents.impact: 3.7 million chat logs, audio recordings, and text transcripts of phone calls from 2024 to 2026 found exposed online without authentication.
